examples.description: post-op wound check, lower leg flap follow-up

transcript.text: How's the leg feeling today, three weeks out from surgery?
Sore but tolerable. I'm taking only Tylenol.
Wound measures 6 by 3 centimetres. Edges are well approximated. No drainage. Granulation tissue visible centrally.
Flap colour matches the surrounding tissue. Capillary refill brisk at under 2 seconds.

claims.text: Three-week post-operative wound check, lower leg.
Patient reports soreness, tolerable, controlled with Tylenol only.
claims.source_quote: Sore but tolerable. I'm taking only Tylenol.

claims.text: Physician noted wound dimensions 6 × 3 cm with well-approximated edges and no drainage.
claims.source_quote: Wound measures 6 by 3 centimetres. Edges are well approximated. No drainage.

claims.text: Physician noted granulation tissue visible centrally.
Physician noted flap colour matches surrounding tissue and capillary refill under 2 seconds.
claims.source_quote: Flap colour matches the surrounding tissue. Capillary refill brisk at under 2 seconds.

examples.description: sacral wound — dimensions L×W×D, tissue percentages, exudate, undermining + periwound, stated assessment + plan

transcript.text: Let's take a look at the wound on your sacrum.
It's been draining a bit more this week.
The wound measures 4 by 2 centimetres, depth 0.5 centimetres. Wound bed is about 80 percent granulation tissue with 20 percent slough. Scant serous drainage, no odour.
Edges show 1 centimetre of undermining at the 12 o'clock position. Periwound skin is intact with mild erythema.
My assessment is a stage 3 sacral pressure injury, improving.
Continue the current dressing, off-loading, and we'll reassess in one week.

claims.text: Wound check of a sacral wound.
Patient reports increased drainage over the past week.
claims.source_quote: It's been draining a bit more this week.

claims.text: Physician noted sacral wound dimensions 4 × 2 × 0.5 cm.
claims.source_quote: The wound measures 4 by 2 centimetres, depth 0.5 centimetres.

claims.text: Physician noted the wound bed as approximately 80% granulation tissue and 20% slough.
claims.source_quote: about 80 percent granulation tissue with 20 percent slough

claims.text: Physician noted scant serous drainage with no odour.
Physician noted 1 cm of undermining at the 12 o'clock position and intact periwound skin with mild erythema.
claims.source_quote: 1 centimetre of undermining at the 12 o'clock position. Periwound skin is intact with mild erythema.

claims.text: Physician stated an assessment of stage 3 sacral pressure injury, improving.
claims.source_quote: My assessment is a stage 3 sacral pressure injury, improving.

claims.text: Physician stated a plan to continue the current dressing and off-loading, with reassessment in one week.
claims.source_quote: Continue the current dressing, off-loading, and we'll reassess in one week.
